children.name: Ignition
children.description: Touch a target and deal 1 heat damage. If the target is a flammable object, ignite it as if by a match. If the target is a creature, requires an attack roll.

children.name: Short Range Ignition
children.description: You can now use your ignition at a range of 6 meters rather than touch.

children.name: Gout of Flames
children.description: All creatures in the cone make a Dodge check vs caster’s attack DC, taking 2 heat damage on a failure, 1 on a success, and 4 on a critical failure.

children.name: Control Flames
children.description: Manipulate an existing flame you can see within 9 meters. You can: Move a flame up to 3 meters (dealing 1 damage on contact with an attack roll), shape a flame into simple patterns, reduce or amplify the intensity of the flame, or snuff out a flame no larger than a  torch.

children.name: Wall of Fire
children.description:
Create a wall of fire 2 meters in height and 1 meter thick which partially obscures sight. The wall can be whatever shape you choose, but must have a total contiguous length of 5 meters or less, and every point along which must be within range.
Any creature that begins their turn touching the wall or comes into contact with it during their turn must make a dodge check against your attack DC, taking 2 heat damage on a fail, 1 on a success, and 4 on a critical failure.
Every turn after the first, you must spend 1 AP concentrating on the effect, otherwise it dissapears.

children.name: Wreathed in Flame
children.description:
Target one object of medium size or smaller, which becomes wreathed in special flames which do not harm the object. 
If the target was a weapon, on a successful it the weapon deals 1 heat damage in addition to the weapons normal damage, and 2 additional damage on a critical hit.
If the target was a piece of armor, anyone who touches the person wearing the armor against their will or attacks them from within 2 meters will take 1 heat damage (after the instigating action).
If target is an object not being held or worn, anyone first attempting to touch it will take 1 heat damage. 
Every turn after the first, you must spend 1 AP concentrating on the effect, otherwise it dissapears

children.name: Small Explosion
children.description: Release a concussive burts of flame. Each creature within the shape (possibly including you and your allies) makes a dodge check against your attack DC, taking 2 heat damage on a failure, 1 on a success, and taking 4 heat damage and being pushed back 1 meter from the center of the burst on a critical failure.

children.name: Combustion
children.description: Put more power into your ignition, allowing it to set a creature alight. On an attack roll, the creature takes 2 heat damage immediately, and 1 persistent heat damage. This damage can be stacked, but a creature cna also spend 2 AP to reduce the persistent damage by 1.

children.name: Pyro-Infusion
children.description: When dealing heat damage, you can spend an additional 1 AP and 1 MP to deal an additional 1 persistent heat damage. This damage can be stacked, but a creature cna also spend 2 AP to reduce the persistent damage by 1.

children.name: Chill Touch
children.description:
Touch one creature or object, and deal 1 cold damage with an attack roll. If the target is a damp creature, you have advantage on the attack roll.
If the targte is a damp object or surface, cover up to about 1 square meter of it in a thin layer of ice, making it slippery. Whenever a creature attempts to move across a slippery surface (once per action), they must make a dexterity check vs your attack DC. On a failure their movement ends, and on a critical failure they also fall prone.

children.name: Cryo Spray
children.description: All creatures in the cone make a Dodge check vs caster’s attack DC. On a success, they become chilled and will begin their next turn with 1 fewer AP. On a failure, they also take 1 cold damage. On a critical failure, they take 2 cold damage instead and their AP is reduced by 2. Any creatures who are damp have disadvantage on this save. Any damp surfaces in the area are covered in a thin layer of ice, as with chill touch.

children.name: Shape Ice
children.description:
You manipulate water or ice into simple frozen shapes, which last until broken or melted. Some examples of how much ice you can shape in one turn are:
- A single polearm, or two shorter simple tools.
-Enough spikes to cover 1 square meter, capable of dealing 1 piercing damage for every meter traversed.
- A 1 meter long bridge of ice, just wide enough to be walked on, and strong enough to support a medium creature.

children.name: Animal companion
children.description:
You can summoning an animal companion. This companion remains until dismissed (as a free action) or killed, and will follow your simple verbal commands to the best of its ability. Attempting to summon another companion will dismiss the first.
While you can choose what physical animal form the companion takes, it will have the same stats. It is a medium-sized creature with +0 in all attributes.
It is a matter of flavor whether you summon a spirit to act as your companion, create a new creature from mechanical parts, train an existing animal, or something else. It is also a matter of flavor whether, when killed, the companion actually dies or is safely teleported to a pocket dimension.

children.name: Companion Trait: Tiny
children.description: When you summon your animal companion, you may choose to make them a tiny creature instead of medium, giving them +2 to their dexterity.

children.name: Companion Trait: Large
children.description: When you summon your animal companion, you may choose to make them a large creature instead of medium, giving them +2 to their strength.

children.name: Companion Trait: Flying
children.description: When you summon your animal companion, you may choose to give them a fly at the same speed their walking speed.

children.name: Companion Trait: Aquatic
children.description: When you summon your animal companion, you may choose to give them a swim at the same speed their walking speed, and give them the ability to breathe underwater.

children.name: Companion Trait: Powerful
children.description: (TODO: Give companion a more powerful attack)

children.name: Companion Trait: Tool Use
children.description: When you summon your animal companion, you may choose to give them the ability to use intricate tools and other things that would require opposable thumbs on the same level as a human.

children.name: Companion Trait: Speaking
children.description: When you summon your animal companion, you may choose to give them the ability to speak and understand any langauges that you know.

children.name: Companion Trait: Telepathic Bond
children.description: When you summon your animal companion, you may choose to give them the ability to speak with you telepathically, up to a range of 900 feet

children.name: Companion Trait: Share Senses
children.description: If you give your companion the telepathic bond ability, you can also percieve through its senses while it is within range. While percieving through its senses in this way, you cannot percieve through any of your own body's sene, but use your own perception skill when making any perception checks through your companion.

children.name: Locate
children.description: (Find the closest generic example of a creature or object type within a relatively short range (any ring, any wolf, etc))

children.name: Hunter's Mark
children.description: You mark a creature you cans see within range as your target. While marked, you have advantage on attacks against that creature. This mark expires after 10 minutes, or once the creature leaves the range. You can only have one hunter's mark active at a time, and applying a second one removes the first.

children.name: HM Partner
children.description: When you mark a creature, you may spend 3 extra AP to designate one ally as a hunter, allowing them to also gain the advantage granted by your hunter's mark grants.

children.name: HM Teamwork
children.description: Rather than costing 2 additional AP to designate one ally as a hunter, you may spend 1 AP per ally you would like to designate as a hunter, each of whom now also gains the advantage granted by your hunter's mark grants.

children.name: HM Pursuit
children.description: Your hunter's mark now has a range of one mile, and a duration of 24 hours. You have advantage on any checks made to track or find the target.

children.name: HM Unending Persuit
children.description: Your hunter's mark now has an unlimited range and duration.

children.name: HM Clairvoyance
children.description: You now always know the general direction of the target of your hunter's mark, roughly how far away they are from you, and you always know what percentage of their HP they are missing.

children.name: HM Extra Damage
children.description: Whenever you damage your marked target, deal an additional 50% of the original damage, rounded up. If you have the HM Partner or HM Teamwork abilities, your designated allies also benefit from this extra damage.

children.name: HM 2 Targets
children.description: You may now have two hunter's marks simultaniously on different creatures. This requires using the ability again, spending all required AP and MP for the second target.

children.name: HM 3 Targets
children.description: You may now have three hunter's marks simultaniously on different creatures.

children.name: HM Swap on Defeat
children.description: If the target of your hunter's mark dies or falls unconscious, you may spend 1 MP and 1 AP as a reaction to reapply the mark to a different creature you can see within range.

children.name: Figment
children.description: (Make someone see an image out of the corner of their eye, or hear a quick sound. They cannot make out details)

children.name: Animate Shadow
children.description: (Your shadow can act semi-independently from you)

children.name: Force Shield
children.description: (Magically defend youself from damage)

children.name: Mage Armor
children.description: (Magically increase your armor long-term)

children.name: Wall of force
children.description: (Create a stationary magical wall)

children.name: Transposition
children.description: (Short range teleport within sight)

children.name: Phase Through
children.description: (Very short range teleport through walls)

children.name: Directional Warp
children.description: (Increase range of phase-through)

children.name: Dimension Door
children.description: (Longer-range teleport in a direction)

children.name: Full Teleport
children.description: (Teleport to any known location)

children.name: Reactive Transposition
children.description: (Teleport at the moment you would get hit)

children.name: Tele-Swap
children.description: (Swap places with a creature within sight)

children.name: Pause Time
children.description: (Double your remianing AP for this round)

children.name: Magic Circle
children.description: (Store a spell in a glyph so it can be activated later)

children.name: Counter Spell
children.description: (Try to stop a spell from being cast)

children.name: Consume Spell
children.description: (Recover some MP while counterig spell)

children.name: Fists of Steel
children.description: (You can treat your unarmed attacks as weapons)

children.name: Long Limbs
children.description: (Longer range on unsarmed strikes)

children.name: Innate Range
children.description: (Unarmed ranged attack)

children.name: Defend Ally
children.description: (When an adjacent ally takes damage, you can take half of that damage instead of them)

children.name: Fighting Instinct
children.description: (Stealth does not grant an enemy advantage on attack rolls against you)

children.name: Sturdy Frame
children.description: (Increased carry weight and bonus to throwing creatures or heavy objects)

children.name: Finesse
children.description: When making an attack with a melee or thrown weapon with the 'light' property, you may make a dexterity check rather than strength for the attack.

children.name: Find the Gap
children.description: Melee weapons with the light property bypass a target's protection value up to your dexterity.

children.name: Distant Precision
children.description: Your 'Find the Gap' ability now also applies to thrown and ranged weapons with the light property.

children.name: Martial Weapon Training
children.description: Attacks with any weapons with the martial trait deal an extra 1 damage.

children.name: Break Concentration
children.description: (Try to stop a mage from casting a spell)

children.name: Returning Weapon
children.description: (Mark two weapons or items at the end of a rest, and call them to fly bac into your hand)

children.name: Recall Weapon
children.description: (Items or weapons teleport instead of flying)

children.name: Reverse Recall
children.description: (You teleport to your weapon instead)

children.name: High-Velocity Return
children.description: (Returning weapon can deal damage in a straight line)

children.name: Projectile Volley
children.description: (Spend extra AP to turn your ranged weapon into an AOE weapon)

children.name: Magic Ammunition
children.description: (Spend AP instead of physical ammo)

children.name: Power Slide
children.description: (Spend some extra initial AP to move at double speed in a straight line)

children.name: Bull Charge
children.description: (Damage all enemies in straight line and knock them back)

children.name: Precise Movement
children.description: (Use dexterity for finesse weapons and climbing)

name: Malus
description: Negative abilities or weaknesses that give you an LP instead of ability_pointsing one

children.name: Dash Attack
children.description: (Move and attack all enemies you some within melee range of)

children.name: Show Off
children.description: (Inspire an ally anytime you get a crit on a skill you're trained in)

children.name: Roll D4's on crits instead of doubling
Return Damage
children.description: (Charge up damage and deal it back in one big attack or spell)
